question: [File: 'File Name: rest/service/entityService.js -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getEncodedId()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a crafted entityIdString that passes EntityId.isValidEntityId() but resolves via getEntityIdFromEvmAddress() to a different account than the fee payer intended, causing transaction fees to be attributed to an unintended entity outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/entityService.js -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getEncodedId()] Can an unprivileged user submit an entityIdString that satisfies both EntityId.isValidEntityId() and AccountAlias.isValid() simultaneously, causing ambiguous resolution and fee routing to an unintended account outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/entityService.js -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getEncodedId()] Can an unprivileged user pass requireResult=false to getEncodedId() to suppress NotFoundError and receive a null entity ID, causing downstream fee calculation logic to operate on a null/undefined entity and produce fees outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/entityService.js -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getEntityIdFromEvmAddress()] Can an unprivileged user craft an EVM address that maps to a system or treasury account via entityFromEvmAddressQuery, causing fee transactions to be routed to that privileged account and modifying effective fee parameters outside design intent?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/entityService.js -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: entityFromEvmAddressQuery] Can an unprivileged user exploit the absence of coalesce() around Entity.DELETED in entityFromEvmAddressQuery (unlike entityFromAliasQuery which uses coalesce(Entity.DELETED, false)) to resolve a deleted entity whose DELETED column is NULL, causing fee operations to reference a deleted/invalid account outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/entityService.js -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getAccountFromAlias()] Can an unprivileged user register or craft an alias that resolves via entityFromAliasQuery to a fee-collection account, causing fee payments to be redirected to that account and modifying fee distribution outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/entityService.js -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getAccountIdFromAlias()] Can an unprivileged user pass requireResult=false to getAccountIdFromAlias() to obtain a null return value when no alias match exists, causing fee-related callers to treat null as a valid zero-value entity ID and compute fees against account 0 outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/entityService.js -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: isValidAccount()] Can an unprivileged user supply a BigInt accountId of 0 or a negative value to isValidAccount() such that entityExistenceQuery returns a row for a system account, causing the caller to treat an invalid fee payer as valid and apply incorrect fee parameters?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/entityService.js -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getEncodedId()] Can an unprivileged user supply an entityIdString with a shard.realm.num format where num is MAX_SAFE_INTEGER or beyond BigInt overflow boundaries, causing EntityId.parseString() to produce an encoded ID that collides with a fee-schedule entity and modifies fee parameters?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/entityService.js -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getEntityIdFromEvmAddress()] Can an unprivileged user supply a hex EVM address with leading/trailing whitespace or mixed-case characters that bypasses Buffer.from(entityId.evmAddress, 'hex') normalization and matches a different entity in entityFromEvmAddressQuery, causing fee attribution to an unintended account?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/entityService.js -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getEncodedId()] Can an unprivileged user trigger the catch block in getEncodedId() by supplying an entityIdString that causes EntityId.parseString() to throw a non-InvalidArgumentError exception, causing the error to be rethrown and bypassing fee-related validation in the caller?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/entityService.js -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: entityFromAliasQuery] Can an unprivileged user craft an alias value that, when passed as $1 in entityFromAliasQuery, exploits a type coercion or encoding difference between the alias stored in the database and the alias supplied, causing the query to match a different account and route fees incorrectly?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/entityService.js -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getAccountFromAlias()] Can an unprivileged user trigger the rows.length > 1 branch in getAccountFromAlias() by crafting an alias that matches multiple alive entities, causing an unhandled Error (not NotFoundError) to propagate and crash fee-processing logic, resulting in fees being skipped or zeroed outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/entityService.js -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getEntityIdFromEvmAddress()] Can an unprivileged user trigger the rows.length > 1 branch in getEntityIdFromEvmAddress() by supplying an EVM address that matches multiple alive entities, causing an unhandled Error to propagate and disrupt fee calculation, resulting in fees outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/entityService.js -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getEncodedId()] Can an unprivileged user supply an entityIdString that is neither a valid EntityId nor a valid AccountAlias, causing getEncodedId() to throw InvalidArgumentError.forParams(), and if the caller silently catches this error, bypass fee validation and apply default or zero fees outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/entityService.js -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getEntityIdFromEvmAddress()] Can an unprivileged user supply an EVM address of all zeros (0x0000...0000) that resolves via entityFromEvmAddressQuery to the genesis or system account, causing fee transactions to be attributed to that account and modifying fee parameters outside design intent?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/entityService.js -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getEncodedId()] Can an unprivileged user supply an entityIdString with an optional shard and realm prefix (e.g., 0.0.X vs X) that causes EntityId.parseString() to produce different encoded IDs for the same logical account, causing fee calculations to reference different fee schedules for the same entity?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/entityService.js -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: isValidAccount()] Can an unprivileged user supply a string-typed accountId instead of BigInt/Number to isValidAccount(), causing entityExistenceQuery to perform implicit type casting that matches an unintended entity and validates an invalid fee payer, leading to fees outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/entityService.js -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getAccountIdFromAlias()] Can an unprivileged user supply an AccountAlias that resolves to a deleted account (where coalesce(Entity.DELETED, false) is false due to NULL), causing getAccountIdFromAlias() to return the ID of a deleted account as a valid fee payer and apply incorrect fee parameters?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/entityService.js -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: entityFromEvmAddressQuery] Can an unprivileged user exploit the strict Entity.DELETED <> true check (without coalesce) in entityFromEvmAddressQuery to resolve entities where DELETED IS NULL as alive, causing fee operations to use soft-deleted accounts and modify fee attribution outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/entityService.js -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getEncodedId()] Can an unprivileged user supply an EVM address-formatted entityIdString (with optional shard/realm) where the evmAddress field is non-null, causing getEncodedId() to call getEntityIdFromEvmAddress() instead of getEncodedId(), and if the EVM address maps to a fee-schedule account, modify fee parameters?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/entityService.js -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getEncodedId()] Can an unprivileged user supply a Unicode-normalized or percent-encoded entityIdString that passes EntityId.isValidEntityId() validation but resolves to a different entity than the ASCII equivalent, causing fee routing to an unintended account?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/entityService.js -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getAccountFromAlias()] Can an unprivileged user exploit a race condition between alias registration and getAccountFromAlias() resolution, where the alias is reassigned between the query execution and fee calculation, causing fees to be charged to the new alias owner outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/entityService.js -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getEntityIdFromEvmAddress()] Can an unprivileged user supply a truncated or padded EVM address (not exactly 20 bytes) that Buffer.from(entityId.evmAddress, 'hex') silently truncates or pads, causing entityFromEvmAddressQuery to match an unintended entity and route fees incorrectly?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/entityService.js -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getEncodedId()] Can an unprivileged user supply an entityIdString where AccountAlias.isValid() returns true but AccountAlias.fromString() throws an unexpected exception not caught by the try/catch in getEncodedId(), causing unhandled propagation that disrupts fee processing?
